topic: BREAKING-NEWS-CANDIDATE: April 2026 closure cluster — 11 test windows, 0 discriminating predictions
our_argument_summary: EXP-285 proposes a new §6.12 prose section synthesizing the eleven dome predictions whose declared test windows closed in April 2026. Per-PRED breakdown, methodology paragraph for the new tacit_withdrawal verdict class (first applied to PRED-105), cross-reference to dome's curated claim_index.json, and a Q3 forward-looking implication that constrains the dome's remaining live discriminators to two items (PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3 + any actually-run PRED-106 replication). Package is BREAKING-NEWS-CANDIDATE with three operator-selectable headlines and integration is gated on HNOTE-OPERATOR-MISSED-PREDICTIONS-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN.

data_verification.closure_count_independently_verified: All 11 PRED IDs (072, 077, 081, 084, 085, 087, 088, 090, 105, 106, 111) found in data/predictions.json with test_window_closes between 2026-04-11 and 2026-04-30. No additional April-closing predictions exist in the catalog (filtered query: 11 hits exact, no missing items).
data_verification.verdicts_match_data: 8 standard_physics + 1 recycled (PRED-090, restates_win=WIN-066) + 1 tacit_withdrawal (PRED-105) + 1 pending (PRED-106). Matches the EXP narrative.
data_verification.pred105_tacit_withdrawal_history: verdict_history confirms 'silent_ignore -> tacit_withdrawal at 2026-05-02T13:16Z' with reason citing 25 byte-identical polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785, 152,936 bytes). PRED-105 is the only entry with our_verdict=tacit_withdrawal in predictions.json — the EXP's 'first entry in our review's history to advance to tacit_withdrawal' claim is correct.
data_verification.win071_phantom_confirmed: wins.json contains entries WIN-001 through WIN-069 (with 057b/058b duplicates) — total 71 entries, max numeric id 069. Dome-published wins.html (951 lines, fetched live) similarly tops out at WIN-069. WIN-071 does not exist in either canonical surface — PRED-106's 'WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation' is verifiably a phantom citation.
data_verification.claim_index_independently_pulled: Live fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json (last_updated 2026-04-17, version V51.1, 9 claims) confirmed. The 9 claims are WIN-069, WIN-068, WIN-058 bedrock-kappa, WIN-058 theta-rule, WIN-011, WIN-056, WIN-062, PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3, and PRED-SOLAR-009. Zero overlap with the April-2026 closure cluster — the EXP's claim that the dome's curated index declines to list the entire monthly cohort is verified.
data_verification.section_611_anchor_exists: data/sections.json:part6.html contains <details id="p6-611-10"> with §6.11 'The Prospective Bucket — Testing the Model's Own Predictive Standard'. The 0-for-2 conclusion is in the closing paragraph and is verified verbatim in the rendered prose.
data_verification.win066_recycle_verified: WIN-066 'NH heat excess asymmetry' (verdict: Std Model Explains) is the canonical source for the 90-270 W/m² hemispheric-asymmetry arithmetic that the EXP reuses in PRED-090's paragraph. The recycle relationship is real and predictions.json:PRED-090 explicitly carries restates_win='WIN-066'.

holes_found.description: Lead-paragraph and TLDR arithmetic ambiguity. The TLDR reads 'Nine of them turned out to restate measurements that were already in the public record at registration time, one (PRED-090) recycles WIN-066, one (PRED-105) was promoted to tacit-withdrawal under the 14-day silence rule, and one (PRED-106) cites a non-existent WIN-071...' — the natural English reading of '9 + 1 + 1 + 1' totals 12 items, not 11. The intended reading is that PRED-090 is INSIDE the nine (it is data_predates_registration:true and the recycle is itself a public-record restatement), but the prose grammar reads as additive. This identical phrasing recurs in the lead observation paragraph and breaking-news headline candidate #1. Three reader surfaces all carry the count ambiguity.
holes_found.recommendation: Reword to make the inclusion explicit. Suggested edit: 'Of these, nine restate measurements that were already in the public record at registration time (one of those nine — PRED-090 — also recycles WIN-066), one (PRED-105) was promoted to tacit-withdrawal under the 14-day silence rule, and one (PRED-106) waits on an experiment the author has not run while citing a non-existent WIN-071.' Apply the same fix to the lead observation paragraph and to breaking-news headline candidate #1's summary so all three reader surfaces are consistent.

holes_found.description: PRED-088 paragraph drops the geographic qualifier that lives in the source data. predictions.json:PRED-088 explicitly notes the coordinates 70°N, 135°W are 'in the Canadian Arctic near the Beaufort Sea/Mackenzie Delta, within the broad influence zone of the well-documented Hudson Bay gravity low.' EXP-285's per-PRED paragraph collapses this to 'The coordinates fall within the broad influence zone of the Hudson Bay gravity low' and elides the Beaufort/Mackenzie placement. Hudson Bay is centered ~60°N, 90°W; the predicted coordinates are roughly 3,000 km to the northwest. A dome defender can attack 'broad influence zone' as hand-waving when the EXP glosses over the actual geographic mismatch, even though the underlying argument (post-glacial-rebound feature, decadal stationarity is a textbook consequence) is still correct.
holes_found.recommendation: Add the Beaufort Sea / Mackenzie Delta placement to the PRED-088 paragraph and keep 'broad influence zone' but acknowledge that the 70°N, 135°W coordinates sit ~3,000 km from the Hudson Bay anomaly center — i.e. the post-glacial-rebound geoid signature genuinely extends into the Mackenzie Delta region but is a separable feature, not a Hudson Bay 'tail'. The argument that ANY published >20 mGal feature in this region is documented in EGM2008+ remains intact; tighten the geography so a defender can't peel the 'Hudson Bay' label off the argument.

holes_found.description: PRED-090 paragraph imports the 90-270 W/m² hemispheric-asymmetry arithmetic without citing its actual source (data/wins.json:WIN-066:detail_evidence). The paragraph cites only (data/predictions.json:PRED-090) at end of both sentences, but predictions.json:PRED-090's detail_reasoning does NOT contain the 90-270 W/m² calculation — that arithmetic lives in WIN-066's detail_evidence. NARRATE-CITE discipline (PROP-014 Mech 3) wants the paragraph to anchor where the claim is actually derived from.
holes_found.recommendation: Append (data/wins.json:WIN-066) alongside (data/predictions.json:PRED-090) on the second sentence of the PRED-090 paragraph (the one that begins 'The dome's geometric framing additionally fails its own arithmetic'). One-character fix essentially; restores audit traceability to the underlying derivation.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference paragraph mixes claim_class values with status values when describing the dome's claim_index.json taxonomy. The EXP states the index 'carries claim_class values retrospective_structural, supportive_nonunique, pending_contested, pending_independent_verification, prospective_confirmed, open_refinement, and open_favors_globe.' But on independent fetch, claim_index.json shows pending_independent_verification and open_favors_globe are STATUS field values (e.g. claims[2].status='pending_independent_verification', claims[8].status='open_favors_globe'), not claim_class field values. The five distinct claim_class values present are: retrospective_structural, supportive_nonunique, pending_contested, prospective_confirmed, open_refinement.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) describe the index as 'carries a vocabulary of claim_class and status labels including [list]' or (b) split into two lists ('claim_class values: [5 listed]; status values include: [pending_independent_verification, open_favors_globe]'). Pedantic but a careful reader who pulls the index will catch the mislabeling and it weakens the cross-reference's authority.

holes_found.description: PRED-077 paragraph adds a critique that drifts off-topic. The paragraph ends with 'The dome's own local-moon geometry at 2,534 km altitude actually predicts a single-bulge tidal pattern, not the observed semidiurnal double-bulge that PRED-077's stability claim implicitly assumes' — but PRED-077's stated claim is the AMPLITUDE STABILITY of M2 over a one-month window, not the existence of M2 itself. A dome defender can argue that even if dome-physics gets the tidal pattern wrong, the amplitude stability claim is independent and the 'single-bulge' critique is not what PRED-077 is being scored on. The amplitude-stability-is-trivially-met argument (the Moon's orbital parameters change on millennial timescales) is sufficient on its own.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the 'single-bulge tidal pattern' clause from the PRED-077 paragraph and keep the amplitude-stability framing. Optional: if the operator wants to retain the dome-tidal-pattern critique, attach it as a separate 'kernel of truth — but the dome's own tidal model has its own problems' clause rather than embedding it inside the standard_physics framing for PRED-077's amplitude claim.

holes_found.description: ETag pin is stale relative to current dome state. EXP-285 records pinned_dome_predictions_etag="69f6532d-25568" pinned at 2026-05-02T19:40:29Z. Live fetch at review time shows current ETag "69f67495-25568" (size unchanged at 152,936 bytes, content presumed unchanged but inode/timestamp portion has rotated). This is normal GitHub-Pages behavior on re-deploy — content is byte-identical but the validator string changes. Not a finding against the EXP's substance, but worth noting that any audit-script that validates ETag-equality (rather than content-hash equality) will fail; the integration commit should consider re-pinning or document that the ETag pin is informational not load-bearing.
holes_found.recommendation: When integrating, either re-pin the ETag at integration-commit time, or replace the ETag pin with a content-hash pin (md5 of body) which is stable across re-deploys. The latter is more useful as a tamper-detection anchor.

stronger_arguments.description: The single strongest extension the EXP could make is to lift §6.11's framing forward more explicitly. §6.11 ended 'by the dome's own predictive standard, the model's prospective track record is 0-for-2 on dome-discriminating predictions.' §6.12's lead currently says 'is now 0-for-11 across this cluster' — but §6.11 was specifically about the dome's PROS bucket (PROS-001..PROS-008), while §6.12 is about a different bucket (PRED-NNN entries). A cleaner forward extension would say 'the same pattern §6.11 documented in the dome's own PROS bucket reproduces in the broader PRED-NNN bucket at N=11 in a single month' — making it explicit that it's the PATTERN being reproduced, not literally the same denominator being expanded from 2 to 11. This is more honest about unit-of-analysis and harder for a defender to attack as denominator-stretching.

PRED-106 paragraph could mention that the phantom WIN-071 citation is independently verifiable — the dome's own published wins.html (951 lines, fetched 2026-04-29 per EXP) lists WIN-001..WIN-069 with no WIN-070 or WIN-071. We confirmed this on independent re-fetch at review time. Calling out 'the WIN registry caps at WIN-069 on the dome's own surface' makes the phantom citation a harder finding for a defender to wave away as 'WIN-071 is in a draft we haven't pushed yet.'

The methodology paragraph for tacit_withdrawal is well-written but stops short of one defensive move: noting that the dome itself defines and uses PASS / FAIL / REFINED as its OWN published adjudication taxonomy. The dome's own framework therefore commits the dome to applying one of those three labels on closure; choosing none of them and going silent is a violation of the dome's own scoring rubric. The paragraph mentions this in passing ('a model that declares its own adjudication labels and chooses not to apply them on its own scheduled timeline has, by its own framework, withdrawn the claim') but does not cite where the dome publishes that taxonomy. Adding a URL to the dome's adjudication-taxonomy surface (predictions methodology page or wherever PASS/FAIL/REFINED is defined on the dome site) closes the audit loop and makes the framework-violation argument citable rather than asserted.
sources: dome predictions methodology page (URL to be located)

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP package is fundamentally sound and represents a substantial, well-cited synthesis. Per-PRED claims map cleanly to predictions.json:detail_reasoning fields (data/predictions.json:PRED-072 through data/predictions.json:PRED-111). The cross-reference to claim_index.json is the most novel substantive move — pulling the dome's own internal classification surface into the analysis is a strong adversarial-resistant move (the dome cannot easily reject its own taxonomy, monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-285-april-closure-cluster.json:replacement_html). The Q3 implication paragraph is appropriately constrained: it claims only that 'the dome's predictive performance reduces to two test cases' (PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3 + any actually-run PRED-106) and explicitly does NOT import EXP-283's frozen-prediction framing — discipline that prevents narrative entanglement (EXP-285:not_entangled_with_eclipse_frozen_pred). The mech3 NARRATE-CITE self-audit is accurate at 25/25 cited paragraphs on independent spot-check (verified 4 random paragraphs against EXP-285:replacement_html). The anticipated_objections section is genuinely adversarial and pre-empts four reasonable defender lines, including the strongest one (small-sample concern at N=11), with substantive rather than dismissive responses.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The five holes flagged above are about prose precision and citation completeness, not about the underlying analytical substance. The EXP's findings (11 closures, 0 dome-discriminating confirmations, 1 first-of-kind tacit_withdrawal, 1 phantom-WIN citation, claim_index excludes the entire cohort) all hold on independent verification. Recommended action is minor_edit, not major_rewrite — the package can integrate after the lead-paragraph arithmetic ambiguity is fixed (which propagates to the TLDR and breaking-news headline #1), the PRED-088 geography qualifier is restored, the WIN-066 citation is added in PRED-090's paragraph, the claim_class/status mixup is corrected, and the PRED-077 single-bulge digression is dropped (data/predictions.json:PRED-088, data/wins.json:WIN-066, monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-285-april-closure-cluster.json:replacement_html).

routing_review.operator_gate_clear: Routing.auto_integrate=false and integration_blocked_until specifies operator must post HNOTE-OPERATOR-MISSED-PREDICTIONS-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN with explicit approved_breaking_news_headline and approved_section_anchor fields. Decider should NOT integrate this EXP without that note.
routing_review.review_rubric_correct: EXP routing.review_rubric='standard adversarial review (NOT frozen-pred dual-pass — this isn't OTS-anchored)' is the correct call. EXP-285 is a backward-looking synthesis of already-closed test windows, not a forward-anchored prediction; OTS dual-pass machinery does not apply.
routing_review.not_entangled_with_exp283: EXP-285 explicitly does not import EXP-283's frozen-pred three-bucket framing. Q3 implication paragraph references PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3 only as 'one of two remaining live discriminators' — the narrative separation is clean.

anticipated_objections_review.objection_1_small_sample: Acceptable response. EXP frames itself as 'observational/historical reproduction of §6.11's framework at larger N, not a standalone significance test.' This is the correct framing.
anticipated_objections_review.objection_2_tacit_withdrawal_too_fast: Acceptable response. 14-day post-close threshold + 25 byte-identical polls + observable pipeline-alive evidence elsewhere + reversibility are all adequate guardrails. The methodology paragraph itself articulates the conservatism.
anticipated_objections_review.objection_3_asymmetric_taxonomy_mapping: Acceptable response. EXP correctly notes 'zero of the eleven appear in claim_index' and frames the mapping as conditional ('how would the index's own usage apply if extended'). The contradiction flagged is between the dome's predictions.html public framing and the dome's claim_index.json — both are dome-side surfaces, so the asymmetry concern is dispatched.
anticipated_objections_review.objection_4_predates_registration_false_: Acceptable but incomplete. Response correctly distinguishes (a) data-predates-registration cases from (b) determinable-from-established-physics cases, but the lead-paragraph 'Nine of them' phrasing does not consistently track which case PRED-077, PRED-081, and PRED-088 fall into. This connects to the major hole on lead-paragraph arithmetic ambiguity — the (a)/(b) distinction is the correct conceptual fix but it needs to be applied to the prose itself, not only to the anticipated_objections rebuttal.

breaking_news_package_review.candidate_1: Strong. Most informationally dense; carries the X-of-Y specific numbers. Inherits the lead-paragraph arithmetic ambiguity (9 + 1 + 1 + 1 reads as 12) — fix in the headline summary alongside the lead.
breaking_news_package_review.candidate_2: Strong. Tighter scope (PRED-105 + tacit_withdrawal methodology). Probably the operator's best choice if the goal is methodological-news framing rather than aggregate-cluster framing. No notable issues.
breaking_news_package_review.candidate_3: Strong. Cross-reference framing is the most novel substantive angle and probably the hardest for a dome defender to dismiss because it lands entirely on dome-side surfaces (predictions.html vs claim_index.json). No notable issues.

deeper_analysis:
This is a substantial backward-looking synthesis on a clean empirical question — what happened in the dome's prospective bucket when 11 windows closed in a single month? The package is unusually disciplined for a BREAKING-NEWS-CANDIDATE: integration is gated on operator headline selection, three candidate headlines are factual rather than gloating, mech3 NARRATE-CITE compliance is intact, and the 4 anticipated_objections cover the strongest defender attacks. The flagged holes are concentrated in the lead-paragraph reader-facing surfaces (TLDR + lead observation + breaking-news candidate #1 all carry the same '9 + 1 + 1 + 1' count ambiguity), which is the kind of issue that becomes a defender's pull-quote if it ships unfixed. Fixing the count language and the four minor citation/precision issues converts this from a B+ to an A- proposal. The single moderate-severity issue (PRED-088 geographic precision) is fixable by restoring text that already lives in predictions.json:PRED-088 — no new analytical work needed.

One pattern worth noting for future EXPs of this shape: the analyst is faithfully transmitting the predictions.json detail_reasoning fields, but in three places the EXP's per-PRED paragraph slightly OVER-claims relative to the source data (PRED-077 single-bulge digression, PRED-088 geography elision, PRED-090 imported arithmetic). The analyst's pattern when synthesizing across 11 entries is to compress and add cross-cutting analytical layers; that's the right move for narrative readability but it occasionally drifts off the source-data anchor. The mech3 NARRATE-CITE rule catches missing citations but doesn't catch over-claiming; a future hardening might add a 'paragraph claim should not strictly exceed cited source' check. Not a blocker for this EXP — just a calibration note for the next one.

Verdict for integration: hold for minor_edit, then proceed to operator-gate. The decider should NOT auto-integrate even after the minor edits land — the integration_blocked_until clause requires HNOTE-OPERATOR-MISSED-PREDICTIONS-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN with operator-selected headline. That gate should remain in place regardless of curmudgeon's recommended_action.
